chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.2785 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application ------------------------------------------ 25.02.2025 Mr. Muhammad Shafiq Abdullah, advocate for applicant Ms. Seema Zaidi, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh ------------------------------------ SHAMSUDDIN ABBASI, J.—Applicant/accused Muhammad Javed son of Sonehar seeks post arrest bail in FIR No.724/2023, registered at P.S. Gulshan-e-Iqbal, Karachi, for offence under Sections 395, PPC, after rejection of his bail plea by Additional Sessions Judge-XII, Karachi East vide order dated 08.04.2024. 2. Brief facts of the case are that on 10.11.2023 at 0940 hours complainant was present along with workers at Godown, when 5 armed persons appeared, out of them four came to godown office and snatched: sale proceeds of flour Rs.236,000/-; from complainant Rs.13,500/-; from workers Muhammad Owais mobile phone Samsung A/54, with Sim Card 03273564064 and cash Rs.29,000/-; from Khalid, Infinix Cell Phone with Sim Card 0315-6938409, from Adnan Oppo Cell Phone with Sim No.03182758228 and Cash Rs.3000/-; from Azhar Iqbal Cell Phone Metrolla with Sim Card 03009233267, from Syed Ali Shah, Nokia Phone with Sim Card 03132891107 and cash Rs.1800/-, thereafter they ran away, such incident was reported at 15 and complainant lodged such FIR at police station. 3. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant/ accused is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that there is no recovery of robbed articles from the possession of applicant and identification parade has been managed by police, therefore, his case requires further inquiry in terms of Section 497, Cr.PC. 4.

Brief facts of the case are that on 10.11.2023 at 0940 hours complainant was present along with workers at Godown, when 5 armed persons appeared, out of them four came to godown office and snatched: sale proceeds of flour Rs.236,000/-; from complainant Rs.13,500/-; from workers Muhammad Owais mobile phone Samsung A/54, with Sim Card 03273564064 and cash Rs.29,000/-; from Khalid, Infinix Cell Phone with Sim Card 0315-6938409, from Adnan Oppo Cell Phone with Sim No.03182758228 and Cash Rs.3000/-; from Azhar Iqbal Cell Phone Metrolla with Sim Card 03009233267, from Syed Ali Shah, Nokia Phone with Sim Card 03132891107 and cash Rs.1800/-, thereafter they ran away, such incident was reported at 15 and complainant lodged such FIR at police station. 3. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant/ accused is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case due to mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; that there is no recovery of robbed articles from the possession of applicant and identification parade has been managed by police, therefore, his case requires further inquiry in terms of Section 497, Cr.PC. 4. On the other hand, learned Additional Prosecutor General Sindh opposed the grant of bail to applicant on the ground that complainant identified the present applicant during identification parade; he has fully implicated him in the commission of alleged offence; that there is CRO of applicant, which shows that applicant/accused is involved in three more cases of like nature and during investigation crime weapon has been recovered from his possession. 5. Heard learned counsel for applicant as well as Additional Prosecutor General Sindh and perused the material available on record. 6. No doubt, applicant/accused is not nominated in the FIR but during investigation he was put to identification parade where complainant has rightly identified him as one of the culprits of alleged offence. Record reflects that he is involved in three more cases of like nature viz. FIR No.608/2022, under sections 397/34, PPC, FIR No.214/2023, under sections 353/324, PPC and FIR No.215/2023 under section 23(a) of the Sindh Arms Act, 2013, all registered at Police Station New Karachi. No case of further inquiry is made out, therefore, instant criminal bail application is dismissed. 7. Needless to mention here that the observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature, the same would not influence the trial Court while deciding the case of the applicant/accused on merits. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
